mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 5.5 percent signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending, leading to negative corporate earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, or the signal produces a negative Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand, forcing equity markets to price in lower near-term revenue growth and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint decline periods show no statistically significant difference in forward returns compared to baseline, with a t-statistic below 1.5 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month contractions in industrial production signal immediate supply chain bottlenecks and inventory drawdowns, triggering short-term risk-off positioning and negative 5-day equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns during industrial contractions exceed positive 0.5 percent in more than 60 percent of cases, invalidating the risk-off mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained CPIAUCSL readings above 230 indicate entrenched inflation that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 65 percent of high-inflation observations, or the signal yields a negative information coefficient over any 36-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment signals labor market inefficiency and wage-price spiral risks, prompting institutional deleveraging and negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Divergence periods yield positive 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of cases with a t-statistic exceeding 1.5, refuting the deleveraging channel.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above 2.0 percent confirms robust aggregate demand, supporting corporate earnings trajectories and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains negative over any 24-month rolling window, or forward returns during high-GDP quarters are statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 100.0 indicates a contraction in manufacturing output that immediately reduces near-term earnings expectations, causing negative 5-day equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day returns are positive in more than 60 percent of triggered instances, or the signal produces a negative cumulative return over a 12-month out-of-sample period.
